title: 1 Speciedaler - Christian IV (Standing King - Type 1a)

obverse.description: Crowned standing figure of King Christian IV in full armor. His left hand is on sword pommel, while his right is on a crowned shield standing on the ground at his right side. King's figure is inside a circle with legend outside. Mintmark at end of legend.
obverse.lettering_translation: King Christian IV, by the Grace of God King of Denmark and Norway, the Goths and the Wends
obverse.picture_copyright: Fritz Rudolf Künker GmbH & Co. KG, Osnabrück und Lübke + Wiedemann KG, Leonberg

reverse.description: Nude female figure representing Fortuna standing on globe, looking left and holding a long flying banner. The figure is within a beaded circle with legend outside. Date is at end of legend.
reverse.lettering_translation: New coin of the City of Gluckstadt
reverse.picture_copyright: Fritz Rudolf Künker GmbH & Co. KG, Osnabrück und Lübke + Wiedemann KG, Leonberg

comments:
This coin has a lot of variants, rarely described in catalogues. The main reference for a correct identification is the work of Knudsen and Nielsen (Knudsen, K. og Nielsen, P.: Møntvarianter og fejlprægninger fra Christian IIII. Kastrup 1982.). They divide the species type into 7 variants. These variants are mainly based on variation in the inscription (Chr. IIII vs. Chr. 4), in number of digits in the year and the coin-master mark.

TYPE: 1 Reverse: Fortuna is very clumsy. The legs are not crossed. The sail is blowing to the right.
1A: K&N 156 A 1623 Schou 11-12 CHRISTIANVS IIII ..... one single mintmark / MO:NO:CIVI or MONE:NO:CIV:
1B: K&N 156 B 1623 Schou 13 CHRISTIANVS 4 / date as I6Z3
1C: K&N 156 C 1623 Schou 14-35 (14, 16, 17, 21, 25, 26, 29, 35); 1624 Schou 18-19, 26 CHRISTIANVS 4 / date as 6Z3/6Z4
1D: K&N 156 D 1624 Schou 20, 23, 25; 1625 Schou 8-9 CHRISTIANVS 4 mintmarks side by side / date as Z4/Z5
1E: K&N 156 E 1624 Schou 24
1F: K&N 156 F 1624 Schou 21-22; 1625 Schou 5-7 CHRISTIANVS 4 mintmarks overlapped / date as Z4/Z5

TYPE: 2 Reverse: Fortuna is made more gracefully than on the smaller coins. The legs are crossed. The sail is blowing to the right.
